baseline.description: Focused Opus-only agent-configuration benchmark baseline for the variants that remain useful after exploratory runs.
notes: Only compare runs that share the same baseline id.
Historical runs without baseline metadata are treated as legacy-unversioned and kept for audit only.
The baseline keeps inherited production guidance with non-operational content trimmed, plus controls for no repository documentation, no skills, no guidance, and minimal guidance.
OpenCode runs are intentionally excluded from this baseline until sequential isolated execution is reliable.

findings: time-aware-agent leads the active baseline by average score (0.792 vs minimal-guidance at 0.712).
baseline-guidance beats unguided-agent on average by 0.030.
baseline-guidance beats unguided-agent on 2/7 challenges, loses on 5/7, and ties on 0/7.
minimal-guidance is ahead of baseline-guidance by 0.018, suggesting the full guidance may contain runtime noise for short tasks.
recommendations: Inspect the largest baseline-guidance losses first: default (-0.201), ambiguous-improvement (-0.117), story-ac (-0.108).
Simplify runtime guidance by moving process-heavy or human-review instructions out of the default agent prompt, keeping only rules that directly improve task execution.
Preserve guidance patterns that helped in the strongest wins: minimal (+0.321), story-ac-with-skills (+0.311).
Rerun the same baseline with more iterations before treating the ranking as statistically stable.
